intro: Calandrinia lockhartii, also known as the red maire, is a plant species native to the mountainous regions of New Zealand. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help soothe and calm the throat and lungs. The bioactive constituents of Calandrinia lockhartii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Calandrinia lockhartii include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues and as a natural cough suppressant.
